vulnerabilities.name: Semgrep: generic.html-templates.security.unquoted-attribute-var.unquoted-attribute-var
vulnerabilities.message: Detected a unquoted template variable as an attribute. If unquoted, a malicious actor could inject custom JavaScript handlers. To fix this, add quotes around the template expression, like this: "{{ expr }}".
vulnerabilities.solution: Review the code pattern and fix according to security best practices.

vulnerabilities.name: Semgrep: javascript.jsonwebtoken.security.jwt-hardcode.hardcoded-jwt-secret
vulnerabilities.message: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).
vulnerabilities.solution: Review the code pattern and fix according to security best practices.

vulnerabilities.name: Semgrep: javascript.express.security.audit.possible-user-input-redirect.unknown-value-in-redirect
vulnerabilities.message: It looks like 'toUrl' is read from user input and it is used to as a redirect. Ensure 'toUrl' is not externally controlled, otherwise this is an open redirect.
vulnerabilities.solution: Review the code pattern and fix according to security best practices.

vulnerabilities.name: Semgrep: javascript.lang.security.audit.unknown-value-with-script-tag.unknown-value-with-script-tag
vulnerabilities.message: Cannot determine what 'subs' is and it is used with a '<script>' tag. This could be susceptible to cross-site scripting (XSS). Ensure 'subs' is not externally controlled, or sanitize this data.
vulnerabilities.solution: Review the code pattern and fix according to security best practices.
